column1: Brice Turang leads the charge today with a steady bat atop the order, a clear platoon edge, and a strong 72% hit probability—plus, he’s in a park that plays fair for hitters. Shohei Ohtani isn’t far behind, bringing his reliable profile and top-of-lineup role into the most hitter-friendly park on the slate, making him a prime pick against a vulnerable arm. Luis Arraez and Roman Anthony both offer lefty-on-righty advantages and favorable lineup spots, rounding out a set of confident, high-floor options for Beat The Streak players looking to keep their streaks alive.
